designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The brand personality centers on "Desert-Luxe"—a fusion of rugged high-desert landscapes and high-end automotive craftsmanship. The target audience is discerning vehicle owners who view their cars as investments and appreciators of artisanal services.

The design style is **Modern Editorial with Glassmorphic accents**. It utilizes high-contrast typography and a precision-engineered grid. To evoke the "high-gloss" finish of a detailed car, the UI employs sharp architectural lines, expansive whitespace, and translucent, reflective layers. The emotional response is one of confidence, cleanliness, and premium exclusivity.

## Layout & Spacing
This design system uses an **asymmetrical 12-column grid** to move away from "templated" looks. 

- **Asymmetry:** Key content blocks should often be offset (e.g., an image spanning columns 1-7 with text starting on column 9).
- **Whitespace:** Use generous vertical spacing (`section-gap`) to allow high-resolution automotive imagery to breathe.
- **Mobile:** Transition to a single-column fluid layout with 20px side margins, prioritizing large "hero" style cards for service packages.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is created through **Reflective Glassmorphism** rather than traditional shadows.

- **The "High-Gloss" Surface:** Use `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)` with a 10% opacity white fill to simulate glass.
- **Thin Borders:** Use 1px "Silver" or "Electric Blue" borders at 20% opacity to define edges without adding visual weight.
- **Lens Flare:** Subtle, low-opacity radial gradients in "Electric Blue" can be placed behind primary surfaces to suggest a light reflection on a polished car surface.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Sharp-edged. Primary buttons use the Terracotta fill with white text; Secondary buttons use a transparent background with a 1px Electric Blue border and wide-tracked uppercase labels.
- **Service Cards:** Use large-scale photography as the background. On hover, a glassmorphic overlay slides up to reveal pricing and details.
- **Inputs:** Minimalist bottom-border only (1px Silver). On focus, the border transitions to Electric Blue with a subtle glow.
- **The "Detailer’s Gauge":** A custom component (progress bar) using the Electric Blue gradient to show detailing progress or ceramic coating longevity.
- **Chips:** Small, sharp-edged boxes with 1px borders for service tags (e.g., "CLAY BAR", "EXTERIOR ONLY").

## Brand & Style
The brand personality centers on "Desert-Luxe"—a fusion of rugged high-desert landscapes and high-end automotive craftsmanship. The target audience is discerning vehicle owners who view their cars as investments and appreciators of artisanal services.

The design style is **Modern Editorial with Glassmorphic accents**. It utilizes high-contrast typography and a precision-engineered grid. To evoke the "high-gloss" finish of a detailed car, the UI employs sharp architectural lines, expansive whitespace, and translucent, reflective layers. The emotional response is one of confidence, cleanliness, and premium exclusivity.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in Santa Fe’s natural landscape but polished for a tech-forward automotive service.

- **Primary (Terracotta):** Used for key brand moments and heritage-inspired accents.
- **Secondary (Electric Blue):** Represents "liquid chrome" and high-gloss water finishes; used sparingly for primary actions and "active" states.
- **Tertiary (Liquid Silver):** Used for metal-like accents and sophisticated line work.
- **Neutral (Slate & Sand):** Deep charcoal forms the canvas (background), while toasted sand provides warmth in secondary text and subtle dividers.

## Typography
The typography creates an editorial, high-fashion feel. **Playfair Display** serves as the authoritative voice for headlines, reflecting heritage and luxury. 

**Hanken Grotesk** provides a clean, technical counterpoint. For utility text and labels, we use a "wide-tracked" approach (increased letter spacing) to enhance readability and create an airy, premium feel. All labels should be set in uppercase to reinforce the architectural precision of the design system.

## Layout & Spacing
This design system uses an **asymmetrical 12-column grid** to move away from "templated" looks. 

- **Asymmetry:** Key content blocks should often be offset (e.g., an image spanning columns 1-7 with text starting on column 9).
- **Whitespace:** Use generous vertical spacing (`section-gap`) to allow high-resolution automotive imagery to breathe.
- **Mobile:** Transition to a single-column fluid layout with 20px side margins, prioritizing large "hero" style cards for service packages.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is created through **Reflective Glassmorphism** rather than traditional shadows.

- **The "High-Gloss" Surface:** Use `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)` with a 10% opacity white fill to simulate glass.
- **Thin Borders:** Use 1px "Silver" or "Electric Blue" borders at 20% opacity to define edges without adding visual weight.
- **Lens Flare:** Subtle, low-opacity radial gradients in "Electric Blue" can be placed behind primary surfaces to suggest a light reflection on a polished car surface.

## Shapes
To maintain the "Precision" and "Architectural" keywords, this design system uses **Sharp (0px)** corners for all primary containers, buttons, and input fields. 

Roundedness is only permitted for "organic" elements like water-droplet icons or profile avatars. The strict 90-degree angles convey a sense of technical mastery and rigorous standards.

## Components
- **Buttons:** Sharp-edged. Primary buttons use the Terracotta fill with white text; Secondary buttons use a transparent background with a 1px Electric Blue border and wide-tracked uppercase labels.
- **Service Cards:** Use large-scale photography as the background. On hover, a glassmorphic overlay slides up to reveal pricing and details.
- **Inputs:** Minimalist bottom-border only (1px Silver). On focus, the border transitions to Electric Blue with a subtle glow.
- **The "Detailer’s Gauge":** A custom component (progress bar) using the Electric Blue gradient to show detailing progress or ceramic coating longevity.
- **Chips:** Small, sharp-edged boxes with 1px borders for service tags (e.g., "CLAY BAR", "EXTERIOR ONLY").

screens.name: projects/7452633150557821770/screens/53c88677742b454285ed72eaab706e13
screens.prompt:
Build a complete, bespoke DESKTOP landing page for 'Santa Fe Mobile Auto Detailing & Pressure Wash'. Visual Style: 'High-Desert Precision'. Dark mode, Playfair Display headers, deep terracotta and charcoal tones with liquid silver accents. Editorial, asymmetrical grid layout. Use glass-morphism and high-contrast typography. Required Sections & Order: 1. Header: Use the TopNavBar component {{DATA:COMPONENTS:COMPONENTS_5}}. 2. Hero: High-contrast serif headline "Precision Automotive Artistry." Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_2}} as a massive hero visual. 3. #credibility: Showcase stats like "12+ Years Experience" and "500+ Ceramic Applications" with minimalist icons. 4. Services/Offerings: "The Precision Suite". Use {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} for Ceramic Coating and {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_3}} for Exterior Precision. Include pricing and durations. 5. About: "Artisanal Care in the High Desert." Narrative copy about the harsh NM landscape and the need for specialized care. 6. Visit (#visit): Map slot with the specific empty div:
. No images or fake maps here. 7. Contact Form: "Reserve Your Session." Fields for Name, Email, Service Selection, and Vehicle Make/Model. Include contact info: (505) 670-2190 and defever22@gmail.com. 8. Footer: Use the Footer component {{DATA:COMPONENTS:COMPONENTS_5}}. Technical Requirements: - Production-ready semantic HTML/CSS. - Responsive design. - Smooth scroll navigation. - No placeholder text (no lorem ipsum). - Distinctive CSS custom properties for the desert palette. - Descriptive alt tags for all images.

screens.title: Santa Fe Mobile Auto Detailing - Official Landing Page

outputComponents.text:
I have completed the production-ready desktop landing page for Santa Fe Mobile Auto Detailing & Pressure Wash.

### Design Highlights:
*   **High-Desert Precision Identity:** The site utilizes the established visual language, blending deep charcoal surfaces with warm terracotta accents and "liquid silver" typography. This creates a high-contrast, premium aesthetic that feels grounded in the New Mexico landscape.
*   **Bespoke Editorial Layout:** Moving away from standard business templates, I've implemented an asymmetrical grid that prioritizes visual impact and typographic hierarchy. The hero section features the signature high-resolution SUV visual to establish immediate boutique positioning.
*   **Integrated Service Suite:** The "Precision Suite" showcases your core offerings—Ceramic Coating and Exterior Precision—using the custom macro and action photography generated specifically for this brand.
*   **Technical Foundation:** The page is built with semantic HTML5 and a robust CSS custom property system for easy maintenance. It includes the required `#visit` map slot, a fully functional "Reserve Your Session" contact form, and smooth-scroll navigation.

Your specific contact details—**(505) 670-2190** and **defever22@gmail.com**—are integrated throughout the experience to ensure high conversion.

outputComponents.suggestion: Make the terracotta accents a bit brighter for more contrast
Add a 'Frequently Asked Questions' section before the contact form
Adjust the hero headline to be even larger
